seoTitle: How to Reverse a Video in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to reverse a video in Canva to create eye-catching effects for social media clips, ads, and creative storytelling.
intro: Reversing a video clip is a quick way to add a creative twist to social content, promotional clips, or storytelling videos. Canva includes a built-in reverse option that avoids the need for separate video editing software. This guide covers how to apply it.

steps.heading: Upload your video clip
steps.body: Add the video you want to reverse to your Canva project's timeline.

steps.heading: Select the clip
steps.body: Click on the video element to bring up its editing toolbar.

steps.heading: Find the reverse option
steps.body: Look for a 'Reverse' or 'More options' menu within the video's toolbar.

steps.heading: Apply and preview
steps.body: Turn on the reverse effect, then play back the clip to check it looks the way you expect.

steps.heading: Trim and export your video
steps.body: Adjust the clip's length if needed, then export your finished reversed video.

faq.question: Does reversing audio come with the reversed video automatically?
faq.answer: It depends on the clip — audio attached to the video may reverse along with it, so check the sound afterward.

faq.question: Is the reverse video feature free in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, basic reverse playback is included in Canva's standard video editing tools.

faq.question: Can I reverse just part of a longer video?
faq.answer: Yes, split the clip first, then apply the reverse effect only to the section you want.

faq.question: Will reversing lower my video's quality?
faq.answer: No, the reverse effect doesn't compress or degrade the footage — export quality depends on your chosen download settings.
